questions.question: What is the primary function of the input-output (I/O) interface?
keyPoints: Enables communication between CPU and external devices
Acts as a bridge between CPU and external devices

questions.question: What are the different types of I/O interfaces?
What is the purpose of buffering in I/O interfaces?
keyPoints: Temporary storage of data during transfer
Prevents data loss and ensures smooth communication

questions.question: What is the role of handshaking in I/O interfaces?
keyPoints: Ensures data is sent and received correctly

questions.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous I/O interfaces?
keyPoints: Synchronous interfaces transfer data at a fixed rate
Asynchronous interfaces transfer data at a variable rate

questions.question: Explain the concept of I/O caching and its benefits in computer systems.
keyPoints: I/O caching stores frequently accessed data in a faster, more accessible location
Reduces access times and improves system performance

questions.question: Describe the role of I/O controllers in computer systems and their importance in managing I/O operations.
keyPoints: I/O controllers manage the flow of data between the CPU and external devices
Regulate data transfer and ensure smooth communication

questions.question: Discuss the concept of I/O scheduling and its significance in optimizing system performance and reducing delays.
keyPoints: I/O scheduling manages the order in which I/O operations are performed
Optimizes system performance and reduces delays
